indicators.note: trending up: RSI 64 toward overbought, %B 0.78 near upper band -> tilt near 0, do not fade strength
RSI 76 overbought, %B > 1 above the upper band -> tilt 0, the wrong side of a fade in a trend

computed.regime_reason: breadth_pct 58 >= 55 AND fear_greed 58 >= 50 (breakout not met: median 1.3 <= 2)
computed.reversion_reason: In an up-trending tape the strategy steps aside: only the laggards DOT and ATOM are oversold enough to fade (tilt 0.92 / 0.72), while the leaders (WBNB/AVAX/INJ) are overbought so their tilt collapses to ~0 and they fall below the entry bar despite strong base scores — mean-reversion refuses to short a trend or buy strength. The risk_on exposure_multiplier 0.4 shrinks the risk budget so only small opportunistic fades are funded; most of the book sits in reserve.

entry: Only small, opportunistic fades in risk_on: exposure_multiplier 0.4 shrinks the risk budget sharply.
Buy only oversold laggards (RSI in (12, 30] AND %B <= 0.20); never fade the trending leaders.
Per-name cap 17%; the remainder stays in the large USDT reserve.
exit: Trim when a held name reverts to the mid (RSI through 55-60 or %B >= 0.50); exit when overbought.
Force-exit at stop_loss 12% or 2.5x ATR, take_profit 25%, or a breakdown (RSI <= 12).
On an upgrade to breakout, step further aside (0.2x); on risk_off rotate to USDT.

actions.reason: reversion_score 0.81 >= 0.65; oversold laggard RSI 31, %B 0.24 (tilt 0.92); small fade within the 0.4x budget
reversion_score 0.68 >= 0.65; RSI 35, %B 0.30 (tilt 0.72); fills the remaining 0.4x budget
reversion_score 0.52 < 0.65: RSI 64 / %B 0.78 near overbought (tilt 0.07) — a leader, not a fade
reversion_score 0.49 < 0.65: RSI 71 overbought, %B 0.92 above the upper band (tilt 0)
reversion_score 0.49 < 0.65: RSI 76 overbought, %B > 1 (tilt 0) — never fade a strong trend

risk_policy.note: Mean-reversion under-performs in trends, so the risk_on 0.4x multiplier keeps the book small and reserve-heavy; surplus over caps falls back to USDT.
